name: Muhammad Ali
abstract: Muhammad Ali ( ah-LEE; born Cassius Marcellus Clay Jr.; January 17, 1942 – June 3, 2016) was an American professional boxer and activist. A global cultural icon, widely known by the nickname "the Greatest", he is often regarded as the greatest heavyweight boxer of all time.

concerts.label: Gala Centennial Salute to Paul Robeson
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Allen
about_quotes.evidence: I laughed when he said that, because to me that sounds like the guys said, “I just saw the films of your fights with Muhammad Ali, beautiful. And I said, “sir, thank you, but you must have somebody else in mind, I never made an album © Hamilton College Jazz Archive -11- with Art Tatum.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: William Russell
about_quotes.evidence: Of course, a boxer, when he gets to be thirty, that's before Ali, Muhammad Ali, too. Always, it was considered any boxer that gets to be thirty has no chance to do anything.

about_quotes.speaker: Larry Gold
about_quotes.evidence: Look at Muhammad Ali. Muhammad Ali came out of that same school.
about_quotes.doc_title: Larry Gold interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 18, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Green
about_quotes.evidence: And he's one of Muhammad Ali's favorite personalities, you see.

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: I remember Muhammad Ali called me in the middle of the night. 'Hey George.' I said, 'Muhammad?', because I knew his voice. I knew him since he was 20.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: It’s lots like going to fight Muhammad Ali, going to New York, because they’re punching at you from all angles, because you don’t know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Danny Barker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
